hypothesis: Given that CHROMA demonstrates a persistent generalization gap between training-paraphrase (ΔE ≈1) and validation (ΔE ≈34) performance, and that this gap persists even with deeper decoders despite successful linguistic variation handling, **incorporating a physics-informed auxiliary loss that penalizes spectral deviation during training will reduce the validation ΔE gap by enabling the decoder to learn optical sensitivity patterns rather than memorizing token-to-spectrum mappings**.
